task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard is named correctly
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title 'UX Correlation Analysis' should be visible at the top of the Graphite dashboard interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Raw Speed' graph is created
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Raw Speed' is visible within the dashboard.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Request Rate' graph is created
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Request Rate' is visible within the dashboard.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Normalized System Behavior' graph is created
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Normalized System Behavior' is visible within the dashboard.

task_completion.description: Verify the graphs are populated with metric data
task_completion.visual_evidence: The graphs should display plotted time-series data lines, indicating that the targets (e.g., averageSeries, derivative, normalize) were successfully configured and are rendering data.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Graphite web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the Graphite web UI (e.g., adding graphs, editing targets) rather than just running terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not manually create the result JSON file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen typing the dashboard JSON structure directly into a text editor or using echo/cat commands to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Verify the graphs are genuinely rendered
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs should show actual rendered images of data lines and axes, typical of Graphite's rendering engine, rather than mockups or static text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'UX Correlation Analysis'. It must contain exactly three specific graphs: 'Raw Speed', 'Request Rate', and 'Normalized System Behavior'. The final result is expected to be saved and verifiable via the Graphite database state.
